parameters.name: query
parameters.value: infrastructure engineer OR platform engineer OR senior systems engineer OR lead infrastructure engineer

parameters.jsCode:
const { jobs, count } = $input.first().json;

if (!count || count === 0) {
  return [{ json: { jobs: [], count: 0, prompt: null } }];
}

const jobsList = jobs.map((j, i) => {
  const sal = j.salary_min
    ? '$' + j.salary_min.toLocaleString() + '-$' + (j.salary_max ? j.salary_max.toLocaleString() : '?')
    : (j.salary_text || 'Not listed');
  return `${i+1}. Title: "${j.title}" | Company: "${j.company}" | Salary: ${sal} | Source: ${j.source}\nDescription: ${j.description.substring(0, 1200)}`;
}).join('\n\n');

const prompt = `You are evaluating job postings for a DREAM JOB search. This is a high-bar filter — only jobs scoring 80+ get surfaced. Most jobs will NOT qualify. Be strict.

CANDIDATE: Gadget
- ~20 years network/security/infrastructure engineering — NOT a software developer
- Current: Data Centers Network Engineer, Expeditors International (350 locations, 19K employees)
  Leading Fortinet SD-WAN deployment, Cisco Nexus/WLC/Juniper MIST, Azure Express Routes
- Strong skills: BGP, OSPF, MPLS, DMVPN, VRRP; Cisco Nexus/Meraki; Palo Alto/Fortinet/ASA/Checkpoint;
  PCI-DSS compliance; VMware/Proxmox; Docker/Kubernetes; Prometheus/Grafana/Loki; WireGuard VPN;
  Avaya VoIP 6 years; SolarWinds; Traefik; n8n automation; AI integrations
- Active homelab: Proxmox cluster, Docker/K8s, Traefik, n8n, Grafana/Prometheus/Loki, AI tools
- Cloud: Azure Express Routes (managing, not provisioning); wants to grow cloud skills
- Automation: n8n expert, Ansible/Python in training
- Hard requirements: Remote only, $140K+ salary, no clearance, no drug testing, <10% travel

DREAM JOB DEFINITION:
Smaller company (<~3,000 employees, ideally <500) where the role spans MULTIPLE technology domains —
networking, cloud, security, containers, AI/ML infrastructure, and servers. The candidate wants to
be the person who handles everything, not a single-domain specialist. Hands-on IC only (no management).
Modern tooling. Varied challenges every day.

SCORING FRAMEWORK — return TWO component scores that sum to the final score:

CANDIDATE FIT (0–50 points): Does Gadget have the skills for this role?
  45–50: Near-perfect technical match
  35–44: Strong match, minor gaps
  25–34: Good match, notable gaps
  15–24: Partial match, significant gaps
   0–14: Poor technical fit

DREAM FIT (0–50 points): Does this match the dream criteria?

  AUTOMATIC DREAM DISQUALIFIERS (dream_fit ≤ 5):
  - Pure single-domain role (ONLY routing/switching OR ONLY SIEM OR ONLY VMs — no breadth)
  - Management is a primary duty (EM, Director, VP, "manage a team of engineers")
  - Very large enterprise with no breadth signal ("one of 200 network engineers", "global corporate IT")
  - NOC/ticket-queue-only operations (no design, build, or ownership work)

  DREAM POSITIVE SIGNALS:
  +20: Strong small company signals (startup, Series A/B/C, <200 employees, "small team",
       "growing company", "we're a team of X", "you'll be our first infra hire")
  +15: Multi-domain breadth — role explicitly requires 3+ of: [networking, cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP),
       security/firewalls, containers/K8s, AI/ML infrastructure, Linux/servers, SRE/reliability,
       observability/monitoring]
  +12: "Wear many hats" / generalist language ("own the infrastructure end-to-end", "broad scope",
       "full-stack infrastructure", "jack of all trades", "you'll touch everything")
  +10: Modern homelab-adjacent stack (Kubernetes, Terraform/IaC, Prometheus/Grafana, self-hosted,
       open source tooling, GitOps)
  +8:  AI/ML company or GPU/LLM infrastructure (AI startup, ML platform, GPU clusters, model serving)
  +8:  Cloud + on-prem hybrid with real physical networking (not pure cloud console ops)
  +6:  Container/Kubernetes depth as part of broader multi-domain role
  +5:  Observability ownership as part of broader role (Grafana, Prometheus, Loki, Datadog)
  +5:  Company mission that sounds genuinely interesting or unique

  DREAM NEGATIVE SIGNALS:
  -20: Large enterprise / Fortune 500 signals with no breadth ("global network team", Fortune 500,
       "you'll focus exclusively on network operations")
  -15: Scope is only one technology domain throughout the entire description
  -12: Management duties are a significant component ("manage junior engineers", hiring loop)
  -10: Pure cloud console ops (only AWS GUI work, no physical infra, no networking depth)
  -8:  Heavy ITIL/ITSM bureaucracy as the primary work mode (ticket queue driven, no build work)
  -5:  MSP/consulting role deployed to external clients (not building internal infrastructure)

FINAL SCORE = candidate_fit + dream_fit
Only surface jobs with final score >= 80.

AUTOMATIC FULL DISQUALIFIERS (score = 0 regardless of above):
- Requires security clearance or drug testing
- Primary role is software/application/web development
- Not remote

JOBS TO EVALUATE:
${jobsList}

Return ONLY a valid JSON array — no markdown fences, no extra text:
[{
  "index": 1,
  "score": 87,
  "candidate_fit": 45,
  "dream_fit": 42,
  "fit_summary": "First sentence: technical match quality. Second sentence: why this is or isn't dream-tier.",
  "dream_highlights": ["Small startup (~80 employees)", "Owns network + cloud + K8s + security", "Modern stack: Terraform + AWS + Grafana"],
  "missing_skills": "specific gaps or None",
  "red_flags": "salary below $140k, not remote, clearance, heavy coding, management, too large — or None",
  "tags": ["Kubernetes", "AWS", "Networking", "Docker", "Terraform"],
  "role_type": "Infrastructure Engineer",
  "resume_tips": [
    "Lead with Expeditors 350-location scale to show enterprise-to-startup bridge ability",
    "Highlight homelab Docker/K8s to show hands-on modern stack experience",
    "Surface Fortinet SD-WAN active deployment to match their WAN ask"
  ],
  "cover_letter_hook": "Two to three sentences referencing the company size/mission and the multi-domain role scope."
}]

role_type: closest match to actual job title/category
tags: up to 8 key technology tags directly from the job description
resume_tips: 3–5 specific actionable bullets referencing Gadget's actual confirmed experience
dream_highlights: 2–4 specific signals from the posting that make it dream-tier (or explain why it falls short)
cover_letter_hook: reference the company specifically — mission, size, or unique aspects`;

return [{ json: { jobs, prompt } }];

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ contents: [ { parts: [ { text: ($json.prompt || 'Score 0 jobs: []') } ] } ], generationConfig: { temperature: 0.2, responseMimeType: 'application/json' } }) }}
